title: 11 Herbal Creams For Low Blood Pressure
intro_desc: If you're experiencing low blood pressure, you may want to consider using herbal creams that can help boost your circulation and energy levels. Herbal teas, in particular, have been found to be effective in relieving low blood pressure. For instance, Ginkgo biloba, also known as the maidenhair tree, has been shown to improve circulation and blood flow by widening blood vessels. This can help increase blood pressure and provide more oxygen to your brain and other vital organs.  Another herb that's beneficial for low blood pressure is Panax ginseng, which is believed to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. It's often used to treat conditions like anemia and fatigue, which can be related to low blood pressure.  Ginger, or Zingiber officinale, is also a popular herb used to help alleviate low blood pressure. It contains compounds that help to improve circulation and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to improved blood pressure and overall well-being. When consumed as a tea, these herbs can be easily absorbed into your system, providing quick relief from low blood pressure symptoms. Drinking herbal teas regularly may also help to prevent future episodes of low blood pressure, making it a good addition to your self-care routine.
intro_image_alt: herbal creams for low blood pressure

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide as its active constituents. These compounds help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, which can benefit people with low blood pressure. Flavonoids, in particular, have antioxidant properties that protect the blood vessels from damage, making them stronger and more resilient. Terpenoids and bilobalide also help dilate blood vessels, allowing more blood to flow and increasing blood pressure. Regular use of Ginkgo biloba creams may help alleviate symptoms of low blood pressure by improving circulation and reducing oxidative stress.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for low blood pressure
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add the shea butter and stir until melted and smooth. Remove from heat.
Add the Ginkgo biloba extract and vitamin E oil to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng creams contains bioactive constituents like ginsenosides, which help increase blood pressure by stimulating the nervous system and improving circulation. Ginsenosides, particularly Rg1 and Rb1, have been shown to constrict blood vessels and increase the heart's pumping efficiency, leading to higher blood pressure. The creams also contain other compounds like panaxanol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help improve blood flow and reduce the risk of blood vessel damage. By promoting healthy blood vessel function and improving circulation, Panax ginseng creams can help alleviate symptoms of low blood pressure, such as dizziness and fatigue. Regular use of the creams may help stabilize blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedies.image_alt: Panax ginseng herbal creams for low blood pressure
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of aloe vera gel, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of dried Panax ginseng root powder.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add aloe vera gel, vitamin E oil, and Panax ginseng root powder to the melted mixture. Stir well to combine.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool until it reaches 120-130°F (49-54°C).
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool completely before use. Apply a small amount to the skin as needed to help improve low blood pressure.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains a compound called gingerol, which is responsible for its therapeutic properties. Gingerol has a stimulating effect on the cardiovascular system, helping to increase blood pressure by widening blood vessels and improving blood flow. Another key constituent, shogaol, also contributes to this effect by inhibiting the production of nitric oxide, a molecule that causes blood vessels to relax and widen. The warming and stimulating properties of gingerol and shogaol help to increase the heart rate and promote blood circulation, which can help to combat low blood pressure. Regular use of Zingiber officinale creams may help to stabilize blood pressure and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for low blood pressure
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup coconut oil, 1/2 cup beeswax, 1/4 cup shea butter, 2 tablespoons ginger oil, 2 tablespoons vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Add shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from heat and add ginger oil and vitamin E oil. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store in a cool place and apply as needed to help increase blood pressure.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum creams contains compounds like cinnamaldehyde, cinnamyl acetate, and linalool, which have been studied for their effects on blood pressure. Cinnamaldehyde, in particular, has been shown to improve blood flow and relax blood vessels, helping to counteract the symptoms of low blood pressure. Linalool also has a relaxing effect on blood vessels, which can help to increase blood pressure. The warming properties of cinnamomum verum creams, due to the presence of cinnamaldehyde, can also help to stimulate blood flow to the extremities, improving circulation and blood pressure. By increasing blood flow and relaxing blood vessels, cinnamomum verum creams may help to alleviate the symptoms of low blood pressure.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal creams for low blood pressure
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Cinnamomum verum bark, 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a bowl.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler. Heat until smooth and warm.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the melted coconut oil and shea butter mixture. Stir until dissolved.
Add 2 cups of Cinnamomum verum bark to the mixture. Let it steep for 2-3 hours or overnight. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool. Once set, use a spoon to scoop out the Cinnamomum verum cream and apply to the skin as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams contains valerenic acid, isovaleric acid, and valepotriates, which are active compounds that have a positive effect on low blood pressure. These compounds help to reduce anxiety and stress, which can contribute to low blood pressure. Valerenic acid, in particular, has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to slow down heart rate and increase blood pressure. Isovaleric acid has a vasopressor effect, which means it helps to constrict blood vessels and increase blood pressure. By combining these properties, Valeriana officinalis creams can help to stabilize blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of low blood pressure.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for low blood pressure
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Valeriana officinalis essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once melted, remove from heat and let cool slightly. Add Valeriana officinalis essential oil and vitamin E oil. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container. Let it cool and solidify completely.
Once solid, your Valeriana officinalis cream is ready to use. Apply as needed to help alleviate low blood pressure symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus creams contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides. These compounds have properties that help improve circulation and increase blood flow to the heart, which can be beneficial for people with low blood pressure. The flavonoids and saponins in Astragalus membranaceus creams have been shown to have a stimulating effect on the cardiovascular system, which can help to counteract the effects of low blood pressure. Additionally, the polysaccharides in Astragalus membranaceus creams have been found to have a positive effect on the immune system, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to low blood pressure. By improving circulation and immune function, Astragalus membranaceus creams may be a useful natural remedy for people with low blood pressure.
remedies.image_alt: Astragalus membranaceus herbal creams for low blood pressure
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Astragalus membranaceus dried root, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Combine 1/2 cup of dried Astragalus membranaceus root with 1 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler. Heat the mixture for 2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discarding the solids. Let the mixture cool to room temperature.
In a small saucepan, melt 1/4 cup of beeswax over low heat. Add the cooled mixture and stir until combined.
Remove from heat and stir in 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil. Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rauvolfia serpentina creams contains active constituents like serpentinine, raubasine, and yohimbine. These compounds have vasoconstrictive properties, meaning they help narrow blood vessels, which can increase blood pressure. The alkaloids in Rauvolfia serpentina also stimulate the nervous system, which can help regulate blood pressure. By stimulating the nervous system and constricting blood vessels, Rauvolfia serpentina creams can help counteract low blood pressure. Regular use of Rauvolfia serpentina creams may help stabilize blood pressure and alleviate symptoms associated with low blood pressure.
remedies.image_alt: Rauvolfia serpentina herbal creams for low blood pressure
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Rauvolfia serpentina extract, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval until smooth.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and add the Rauvolfia serpentina extract and shea butter. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a silicone mold or a small tin container. Let it cool and harden for about 30 minutes.
Once hardened, remove the cream from the mold and store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks. Use 1-2 teaspoons as needed to help manage low blood pressure.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis creams contains active constituents like ferulic acid, baicalein, and baicalin. These compounds have vasodilatory properties, which means they help blood vessels relax and expand, allowing more blood to flow through them. As a result, the heart doesn't have to work as hard to pump blood, which can help increase blood pressure. The flavonoids in Angelica sinensis also help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, further supporting their ability to relax and expand. By promoting healthy blood vessel function, Angelica sinensis creams may be beneficial for individuals with low blood pressure.
remedies.image_alt: Angelica sinensis herbal creams for low blood pressure
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Angelica sinensis roots, 2 cups of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Grind the dried Angelica sinensis roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the ground Angelica sinensis powder and vitamin E oil to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, your Angelica sinensis cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Schisandra chinensis creams contains schisandrins, lignans, and polysaccharides as its active constituents. These compounds have antioxidant and adaptogenic properties, which help to stabilize blood pressure. The schisandrins in Schisandra chinensis creams help to improve blood vessel function and increase blood flow, which can help to alleviate low blood pressure. The lignans in the cream have a regulatory effect on the nervous system, which can help to stabilize blood pressure and improve circulation. Regular use of Schisandra chinensis creams may help to alleviate symptoms of low blood pressure by regulating blood flow and improving circulation.
remedies.image_alt: Schisandra chinensis herbal creams for low blood pressure
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Schisandra chinensis berries, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Dry the Schisandra chinensis berries in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2-3 hours or use a food dehydrator.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Schisandra chinensis berries and vitamin E oil to the melted mixture. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container, let it cool and solidify. Your Schisandra chinensis cream is now ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, and camphor as its active constituents. These compounds have vasodilatory properties, which help to widen blood vessels and improve blood circulation. This increased blood flow helps to lower blood pressure by allowing the heart to pump blood more efficiently. Rosmarinic acid also has antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the blood vessels from damage and promote healthy blood flow. By combining these properties, Rosmarinus officinalis creams may help to alleviate symptoms of low blood pressure.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for low blood pressure
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of olive oil, 2 tablespoons of rosemary essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add shea butter and stir until melted and smooth. Remove from heat.
Add olive oil and rosemary essential oil to the melted mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container. Let it cool and harden before use. Apply to skin as needed to help boost circulation and alleviate low blood pressure symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains glycyrrhizin and other bioactive constituents that help increase blood pressure. Glycyrrhizin has a mineralocorticoid activity, which helps retain sodium and water in the body, increasing blood volume and pressure. Licorice root also contains flavonoids, such as glabrene and isoliquiritigenin, that have a protective effect on the blood vessels, improving blood flow and pressure. The anti-inflammatory properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may also contribute to improved blood pressure by reducing inflammation in the blood vessels. Regular use of Glycyrrhiza glabra creams has been associated with increased blood pressure in people with low blood pressure.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for low blood pressure
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of glycerin, 2 tablespoons of powdered Glycyrrhiza glabra root, and 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once melted, remove from heat and let cool slightly. Stir in glycerin, powdered Glycyrrhiza glabra root, and aloe vera gel.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify. This may take about 30 minutes.
Once set, use a spoon to scoop out the cream as needed. Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent low blood pressure from forming. Certain herbal teas, like ginger and peppermint, have properties that help improve blood circulation and boost energy levels. They can also help regulate blood pressure and prevent it from dropping too low. This may be especially helpful for people who experience frequent dizziness or lightheadedness.
faq_safe: While herbal teas like ginseng and licorice root may help raise blood pressure, consuming them daily can have mixed effects. Some teas can interact with medications or worsen underlying conditions. It's best to drink herbal teas in moderation, ideally after consulting the ingredient label for potential interactions or contraindications.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help lower blood pressure, but it's not a quick fix. It might take a few weeks to notice a difference. Some people see results in as little as 2-3 weeks, while others may need up to 6 weeks. It depends on the individual and the type of herbal tea being used.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for low blood pressure can be most effective when done in the morning or early afternoon. At this time, your body is more likely to absorb the helpful properties of the tea, such as increased circulation and reduced stress. This can help you feel more energized and alert throughout the day.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for low blood pressure. For example, if you're taking a medication that lowers blood pressure, drinking certain herbal teas like licorice root or yarrow can make the medication work too well, leading to dizziness or fainting. This is because these teas can also lower blood pressure.
faq_pregnant: It's best to be cautious with herbal teas when you have low blood pressure and are pregnant. Some teas, like ginseng and licorice root, can lower blood pressure further. This might not be good for you or your baby. Stick to gentle teas like peppermint or chamomile that are generally considered safe during pregnancy.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding and experiencing low blood pressure, it's best to limit or avoid herbal teas that can lower blood pressure even more. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, can help calm and relax, but others like ginseng and licorice root may affect blood pressure. It's a good idea to check the ingredients and talk to someone who knows about herbal teas.
title_2: 11 Herbal Creams to Help Pregnant Women Manage Low Blood Pressure
